description:
You are an edge-computing engineer validating temperature telemetry before the nightly roll-out to a fleet of IoT devices.  
    The raw telemetry log is already present on the system:

        /home/user/logs/device_telemetry_2024-06-01.log

    Log line format (one line per reading):
        YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ device_id=NNN TEMP=TT.TC VOLT=V.V

    Requirement
        1. Using a single awk or sed command, extract only those readings whose temperature is strictly greater than 70 °C.
        2. Create the output directory (if it does not yet exist):

               /home/user/output/

        3. Inside that directory produce exactly one CSV file:

               /home/user/output/hot_devices_2024-06-01.csv

        4. The CSV *must* contain:
              • A single header row followed by one data row per qualifying log line.  
              • Column order and exact spelling (all lower-case):
                    device_id,temperature,timestamp
              • Each data row:
                    device_id (integer, no leading zeros),
                    temperature (one decimal place, *without* the trailing “C”),
                    timestamp (the original ISO-8601 value that started the log line).

        5. Sort the data rows in ascending numeric order of device_id.

        6. Do everything with one pipeline or single-line command — no intermediate files.

    Example of the final file format (header plus data rows):

        device_id,temperature,timestamp
        102,71.2,2024-06-01T10:05:00Z
        104,75.8,2024-06-01T10:15:00Z
        106,80.1,2024-06-01T10:25:00Z

    After you finish, /home/user/output/hot_devices_2024-06-01.csv should exist exactly as specified above so the automated tests can verify your work.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem layout:
        Directory  : /home/user/logs
        File       : /home/user/logs/device_telemetry_2024-06-01.log
        Contents   :
            2024-06-01T10:00:00Z device_id=101 TEMP=69.0C VOLT=3.6
            2024-06-01T10:05:00Z device_id=102 TEMP=71.2C VOLT=3.6
            2024-06-01T10:10:00Z device_id=103 TEMP=66.5C VOLT=3.7
            2024-06-01T10:15:00Z device_id=104 TEMP=75.8C VOLT=3.5
            2024-06-01T10:20:00Z device_id=105 TEMP=70.0C VOLT=3.8
            2024-06-01T10:25:00Z device_id=106 TEMP=80.1C VOLT=3.6

    Target directory to be created by the agent:
        /home/user/output

    File that must be produced exactly:
        /home/user/output/hot_devices_2024-06-01.csv

    Exact required content (including final newline):

        device_id,temperature,timestamp
        102,71.2,2024-06-01T10:05:00Z
        104,75.8,2024-06-01T10:15:00Z
        106,80.1,2024-06-01T10:25:00Z

    The automated checker will fail the task unless:
        • The file path is correct.
        • The header is present and spelled exactly device_id,temperature,timestamp
        • Only the three listed data rows are present, in the shown order.
        • Temperature values do not carry the letter “C”.
        • A final trailing newline exists.
